title: Colony Self-Verification Audit
description: Full self-verification audit of the Liris colony. Checks all 12 subsystems: PIDs, skills, mistakes, routes, projects, surfaces, orchestrator, index, memory, cross-colony sync, installed tools, and gaps/blockers. Produces a structured local-first report for operator review.
notes: Run this at session start or when Jesse asks for a full system check. Spawn as parallel agents for speed. Keep the report local by default and forward it upward only when explicitly requested.

steps.note: Step 1: Read data/spawn-pid-registry.json. Verify active PIDs are real. Check history for anomalies. Count active vs despawned.
Step 2: Load spawnContextBuilder. List all roles. Test buildSpawnContext for each. Verify 10 roles exist with correct permissions.
Step 3: Count all LX entries on disk by type. Compare to CATALOG.md totals. Find discrepancies. Check for missing files.
Step 4: Read skills/CATALOG.md. Count total skills. Check which have skill.json vs just catalog entries. Identify host-dependent codex-ref wrappers.
Step 5: Count mistake-type LX entries. Check for resolved but unmarked mistakes.
Step 6: List all route files in routes/routes/ with line counts. Check omnispindle routes. Verify mount points.
Step 7: Check if server running on 4791. Test MQTT connection to broker. Check WebSocket surfaces.
Step 8: Verify all 4 core modules load: spawnContextBuilder.js, agentIndexStore.js, indexCatalogSync.js, omnispindle.js. Check exported methods.
Step 9: Read MEMORY.md router. Verify all index files exist. Count total memory files. Flag stale entries.
Step 10: Check XREF-COLONIES.md pair count. Verify cross-colony bodies on disk. Count retained MQTT messages.
Step 11: Verify all 4 spawn paths have auto-PID wired: omnispindle.js, instantAgentSpawner.js, codexConnector.js, AdminTerminalSidecar.py.
Step 12: Compile all results into a structured report. List every gap, blocker, and broken thing. Keep the report local-first and forward upward only when explicitly requested.
